settings.title: Ethos & Transparency
settings.text:
Most of our repairs are quote-based, with availability determined by our technicians’ expertise and the availability of necessary parts. In some cases, we may not be able to offer a repair due to various factors. When that happens, there is no charge, and we’ll do our best to suggest alternative options.

Our goal is to make camera repairs fair, affordable, and transparent. Every quote includes clear pricing, and we're always happy to provide guidance on whether a repair is worth pursuing. In some situations, the cost of repair may exceed the value of purchasing a comparable replacement — if that’s the case, we’ll let you know and recommend trusted alternatives.

settings.title: Delivery details
settings.text: Repairs can be delivered back to you by request only. We are not responsible for secure shipping and we suggest requesting signature.

settings.title: What can you service?
settings.text:
For a full list of our servicing information please see our blog post

We repair most cameras. If you have a special case, you should just email us at repairs@brooklynfilmcamera.com. To give you a better scope, here are some of the services and issues we handle:

  • SLR jams
  • Squeaks and lubrication issues
  • Oil cleaning and removal
  • Shutter speed adjustments
  • Dial repairs and calibration
  • Stuck shutters
  • Broken external or internal pieces
  • General cleanings
  • Full CLA services
  • Aperture smoothness adjustments
  • Aperture oil cleaning
  • Fungus and mold removal
  • Wiring replacement
  • Meter replacement and repair
  • Re-soldering connections
  • Electrical board repairs
  • ...and more

We commonly service cameras from:

  • Canon (AE-1, A-1, F-1, T-series, EOS film bodies)
  • Nikon (FM/FE, FA, F2/F3, Nikkormat, AF film bodies)
  • Mamiya (645, RB67, RZ67, C-series TLR)
  • Pentax (K1000, Spotmatic, MX/LX, 67)
  • Minolta (SRT-series, X-series, Maxxum/Alpha)
  • Olympus (OM-series, Pen F, OM-2/OM-4)
  • Yashica (Mat TLR, FX/FR SLR)
  • Konica (Autoreflex, T-series)
  • Ricoh (KR-series, XR-series)
  • Topcon, Praktica, Exakta mechanical SLRs
  • Rollei TLR (Rolleicord/Rolleiflex — limited scope)
  • Bronica (SQ/ETR/GS — selected services)
  • Voigtländer, Zeiss Ikon 35mm SLRs (case dependent)
  • And many other classic Japanese and European cameras

Note: Some complicated and high-end cameras such as certain Leicas, some FUJI GA series, and the Contax series can only receive limited repairs — we do not currently offer full CLAs or certain repairs for these models. We are actively expanding our department to support a broader range of advanced cameras.

settings.title: What's a CLA
settings.text:
A CLA (clean, lubricate, adjust) is one of the best ways to keep your camera running smoothly and extend its lifespan. While a CLA sometimes resolves specific issues, its primary purpose is preventative maintenance: restoring your camera to reliable working order and ensuring consistent performance for years to come.

Please read about CLA's here

settings.title: Pricing
settings.text:
All of our repairs are quote-based, but below are some examples of common repairs and past pricing. Please note that just because you are experiencing a similar issue, it does not guarantee the same price. Many factors — such as parts availability, the extent of damage, and model differences — determine the final cost.

See better breakdown here

Camera Repair Pricing
- Full CLA — 35mm SLR — Complete service (clean, lubricate, adjust, and calibrate) — $175–225
- Single Issue Repair — Stuck Gear, Jammed Shutter, or Similar — Correction of one accessible mechanical problem — $50–100
- Light Seal Replacement — Removal of deteriorated seals and installation of new foam/seals — $85–125
- Leatherette Replacement — Removal of old covering and installation of new leatherette — $55–125
- Canon “Cough/Squeak” Repair — Lubrication of shutter/mirror mechanism to eliminate squeak — $75
- Lubrication & Minor Adjustments — Basic service for addressing stiffness or other small lubrication issues — $75–150

Camera Repair Pricing
- Full CLA — 35mm SLR — $175–225
- Single Issue Repair (Stuck Gear, Jammed Shutter, or Similar) — $50–100
- Light Seal Replacement — $85–125
- Leatherette Replacement — $55–125
- Canon “Cough/Squeak” Repair — $75
- Lubrication & Minor Adjustments — $75–150

Lens Repair Pricing
- Full CLA — 50mm & below — $125–165
- Full CLA — Medium Format — $165–275
- Full CLA — 85mm & Specialty/Zoom Lenses — $200–330
- Stiff Focus Repair — $100–165
- Aperture Repair (Oily or Stiff Blades) — $55–110
- Fungus Cleaning — $75–165
- Sand Removal — $55–110
